question_text: Which of the following best describes the pattern of a magnetic field around a bar magnet?

options.label: A
options.text: Field lines form circles around the middle of the magnet

options.label: B
options.text: Field lines curve from the north pole to the south pole, outside the magnet

options.label: C
options.text: Field lines point inward toward the centre of the magnet from all directions

options.label: D
options.text: Field lines only exist at the two poles and nowhere else

explanation: Magnetic field lines always flow from the north pole to the south pole on the outside of a magnet. They form a pattern that curves around the magnet, which is why magnets can affect objects even when they're not touching.
